meta.title: EVE Medals | Achievement Verification for EVE Frontier
meta.description: EVE Medals turns indexed EVE Frontier activity into verifiable achievement states, on-chain medals, Warrior profiles, and shareable cards.
meta.socialDescription: From indexed activity to medals, Warrior profiles, and shareable honor.

hero.title: Turn Frontier Activity into Verifiable Honor
hero.subtitle: EVE Medals turns indexed EVE Frontier activity into achievement states, on-chain medals, Warrior profiles, and shareable cards, so player contribution can be verified, displayed, and carried beyond the game session.
hero.nextBody: Connect a wallet, inspect what the system already recognizes, then decide whether to keep pushing progress or claim immediately.
hero.postureBody: This is not a static Web3 poster page. It is a working flow around Chronicle scanning, on-chain medals, and public-facing player identity.
hero.previewTitle: See what was indexed, how far it is from the threshold, and whether it is ready to claim.
hero.previewRecentBody: Once the timeline is recognized, the signal maps straight into medal progress and claim readiness.

solution.title: A clear product loop, not a wall of explanation.
solution.body: The homepage should explain the loop directly: inspect indexed activity, understand achievement state, then decide whether the player can claim.

steps.connectDescription: Use a Sui wallet as the player identity anchor. Chronicle builds the profile around that address.
steps.scanDescription: Pull jumps, anchors, kills, and infrastructure actions into a readable Frontier achievement view.
steps.claimDescription: Only threshold-complete achievements unlock claim. The result is not self-reported status, but a verifiable honor layer.

live.title: This is the player’s real-time chronicle.
live.body: After connecting a wallet, the player first sees what the system has already indexed, then decides whether to continue progressing or claim an available medal.

notes.network: The default verification target is testnet unless another supported network is explicitly selected.
notes.preview: Without EVE_EYES_API_KEY, the app falls back to preview mode while still explaining progress.
notes.claim: Without a package ID for the active network, scanning still works but on-chain claim stays disabled.

activity.title: Show the player what they are actually chasing.
activity.body: The easiest tracks to understand are movement, infrastructure, and combat. The homepage should explain the goal, the threshold, and the evidence source without dumping a config table.
activity.objectiveBody: A player should answer three questions in under a minute: what was recognized, how far away the threshold is, and why claim is or is not available.
activity.courierDetail: Sustained movement becomes a visible logistics trace instead of just another screenshot.
activity.pioneerDetail: Anchoring a network node or storage unit is the signal that a player actually left infrastructure behind.
activity.bloodlustDetail: Only confirmed killmail attacker records move combat medal readiness forward.
activity.bloodlustSource: Confirmed attacker records drive combat medal readiness.

trust.title: A player does not care about style unless they can trust the system.
trust.body: The product has to explain data source, behavior class, threshold logic, and the on-chain result. If a button is enabled or disabled, the homepage should make that understandable.

cards.dataSourceDetail: The public window supports preview mode and the keyed path supports deeper history. The product is not inventing activity on the frontend.
cards.behaviorDetail: The current medal system is centered on movement, anchoring, combat confirmation, and adjacent infrastructure activity.
cards.thresholdDetail: Each medal exposes a visible threshold so the player can see what is missing instead of staring at a disabled button.
cards.chainDetail: Claim only opens after threshold completion, turning Frontier history into a persistent on-chain identity signal.

demo.title: A loop that can be shown live in under a minute.
demo.body: The strongest demo path is simple: show indexed activity, open a medal share card, then return through the verification entry point.

steps.oneBody: Start with a wallet that already has meaningful activity and show what is locked, verified, claimable, or claimed.
steps.twoBody: Use the Medal share dialog to show that the card is already designed for social distribution.
steps.threeBody: Use the share link or QR path to land back in the product instead of ending on a static image.

cta.title: See the product loop in action
cta.body: Explore how indexed player activity becomes medal state, public profile context, and shareable reputation assets.

score.title: One number that translates Frontier standing at a glance.
score.body: Each medal contributes to a visible score. Higher-rarity honors carry more weight, verified-but-unclaimed medals count less, and a full bound set receives a bonus. The display is normalized to 0–10,000 so players can compare cleanly.

warrior.title: A public-facing Frontier identity, not just a local profile page.
warrior.body: Each player can generate a Warrior profile that combines score, medals, wallet context, and public-facing proof surfaces. It gives others a way to inspect contribution without trusting self-reported status.

useCases.allianceBody: Share a card with fleet leadership to show cross-domain capability with more than self-reported claims.
useCases.employerBody: Let corps or collaborators inspect a player-facing record instead of trusting screenshots alone.
useCases.communityBody: Create a persistent and portable honor surface around what a player has actually done.

warrior.connectHint: Connect a wallet to open the live Warrior profile.

environment.missingPackageTitle: No public medals package is configured for this network yet
environment.missingPackageBody: Activity scanning still works. On-chain claim simply stays disabled until one of these environment variables is configured:
environment.databaseBody: Without DATABASE_URL, wallet connections do not sync player profiles into SQL. Scanning and frontend rendering are unaffected.
environment.previewTitle: The app is sampling from the public indexing window
environment.previewBody: Without EVE_EYES_API_KEY, the dashboard scans only the public activity window. Claim logic still works, but deeper history can be incomplete.

networkSupport.label: network mismatch
networkSupport.title: The current wallet network {network} is not supported by this Chronicle runtime
networkSupport.body: Switch the wallet to one of the supported networks:

warriorIndex.title: Connect Your Wallet
warriorIndex.body: Connect your wallet to view your Warrior profile and Chronicle medals.

warriorNotFound.title: Void Drifter
warriorNotFound.body: The address you entered is invalid or has no activity on record.

warriorShareDialog.body: This profile card carries the warrior summary, medal highlights, and an embedded QR code that sends viewers straight back to the live warrior page.

notes.qr: Scan the QR code on the card to reopen the live warrior page with the selected network in the URL, then continue into medal verification from there.
notes.discord: Discord still uses the safe fallback: copy first, then open the chat surface so the link can be pasted without relying on unsupported deep share APIs.

toast.copyFailed: Failed to copy the warrior link
toast.discordCopied: Warrior link copied. Paste it into Discord.

panel.explorerBody: The chain interaction is still mocked, but this panel exposes the same transaction checkpoints, objects, and gas surfaces the real explorer flow should reveal.

wallet.title: Approve {action}
wallet.body: Confirm the mock wallet request for {medalTitle}. Once approved, the interface will run the staged {action} pipeline, then settle into an explorer-style receipt.
wallet.note: This is still a mock wallet surface. It does not touch a live chain, but it mirrors the approval rhythm a real wallet should expose before sign-and-submit.

prepare.label: PTB scope
prepare.detail: The transaction block is assembled around {medalTitle}, the target registry/template objects, and the current wallet.

sign.label: Wallet approval
sign.detail: The wallet confirms intent first, then signs the transaction bytes before any broadcast step begins.

finalize.label: Explorer receipt
finalize.detail: After broadcast and checkpoint finality, the UI preserves digest, object, gas, and settlement surfaces for review.

proofVerified.label: Proof verified
proofVerified.detail: Chronicle proof material for {medalTitle} has been validated and accepted into the transaction plan.

transactionAssembled.label: Transaction assembled
transactionAssembled.detail: The PTB has been composed with the required registry, template, and wallet targets.

walletApproved.label: Wallet approved
walletApproved.detail: The wallet has approved signing, so the payload is ready for broadcast.

medalBound.label: Medal bound
medalBound.detail: {medalTitle} has been written into a soulbound medal object and linked to the destination wallet.

claimTicketConsumed.label: Claim ticket consumed
claimTicketConsumed.detail: The mock claim ticket has been marked as spent so the same claim cannot execute twice.

prepare.label: Build Mint PTB
prepare.detail: Assemble the public mint call for {medalTitle}, then lock the template object and recipient wallet.

simulate.label: Dry Run
simulate.detail: Pre-execute the Move call to estimate gas, object writes, and storage rebate before signing.

sign.label: Wallet Sign
sign.detail: Simulate wallet approval and signature generation so the payload is ready for broadcast.

submit.label: Broadcast Tx
submit.detail: Broadcast the signed transaction into the mock testnet mempool and wait for ordering.

finalize.label: Mint Finalized
finalize.detail: Checkpoint confirmation has landed. The medal object is written and ownership is bound to the current wallet.

prepare.label: Verify Proof
prepare.detail: Verify the claim ticket, proof digest, and signer payload for {medalTitle} before building the transaction.

simulate.label: Build Claim PTB
simulate.detail: Assemble the claim transaction block around the registry, claim ticket, and target wallet.

sign.label: Wallet Sign
sign.detail: Simulate wallet approval and signature generation for the claim payload.

submit.label: Broadcast Tx
submit.detail: Submit the signed claim transaction into the mock mempool and wait for execution ordering.

finalize.label: Claim Settled
finalize.detail: Checkpoint confirmation is complete. The medal bind state is updated and ready for sharing.

home.title: The homepage should already speak the same wallet-to-explorer language.
home.body: Even before a live wallet interaction begins, the product should explain the same sequence the user will later see in Chronicle and Warrior: build the transaction, approve in wallet, broadcast it, then settle into a public receipt.

steps.prepare: The player sees exactly what medal action is being assembled and why the transaction exists.
steps.sign: Approval is explicit. The wallet step should feel like a real sign gate instead of an invisible loading state.
steps.submit: Once approved, the transaction moves through a broadcast phase instead of teleporting straight to success.
steps.finalize: The final state is a shareable explorer-style receipt with digest, checkpoint, gas, and object surfaces.

share.body: This share surface is the continuation of the same mock on-chain flow. The medal is settled first, then packaged into a reusable card and handed off into social channels.

notes.handoff: The QR path and share URL reopen the live medal verification page, so this share layer remains attached to live state instead of ending on a dead image.
notes.social: X, Telegram, and Discord are part of the same post-mint handoff. The narrative should feel like one flow, not a separate demo scene.

receiptLocked.label: Receipt locked
receiptLocked.detail: {medalTitle} already has a settled transaction receipt, so the share asset can point back to a concrete on-chain-style outcome.

assetPrepared.label: Asset prepared
assetPrepared.detail: The card renderer packages the medal state, QR path, and social preview format into a reusable distribution asset.

socialHandoff.label: Social handoff
socialHandoff.detail: Platform actions launch directly from the medal receipt context instead of switching to a disconnected demo narrative.

medalShareDialog.body: This share card links straight to the medal verification page. The destination page reloads Chronicle evidence and current Sui medal ownership for the target wallet.

notes.qr: Scan the QR code on the card to reopen the live medal verification page. The page recalculates Chronicle evidence and current medal state instead of showing a static screenshot.
notes.discord: Discord uses a safe fallback: the dialog copies the medal link first, then opens Discord so you can paste it directly into chat.

toast.copyFailed: Failed to copy the medal link
toast.discordCopied: Medal link copied. Paste it into Discord.

bound.label: Chain Bound on Sui
bound.summary: This medal is already bound to the current wallet on-chain.

verified.label: Chronicle Verified
verified.summary: Chronicle has verified the activity, but the medal is not bound on-chain yet.

locked.label: Not Yet Verified
locked.summary: Chronicle has not indexed enough evidence for this medal yet.

medalPage.summarySuffix: This page reloads the latest Chronicle snapshot and Sui ownership state for the target wallet each time it opens.
medalPage.evidenceFallback: Chronicle has not published an evidence string for this medal yet.
medalPage.liveNote: If this link came from a social preview card, the destination is not a static screenshot. It recalculates the Chronicle snapshot and current on-chain ownership state when opened.

toast.newClaimable: New on-chain achievements are ready to claim.
toast.mockMintReady: Galactic Courier mock mint complete. Share it next.

errors.packageMissing: Current network has no medals package configured
errors.registryMissing: Shared medal registry has not been discovered yet
errors.claimTicketMissing: This achievement does not have a valid claim ticket
errors.templateMissing: This medal has no active on-chain template

connect.title: Connect a wallet so the system knows whose chronicle to write.
connect.body: Once the wallet is connected, Chronicle pulls the player snapshot on the active network, checks Eve Eyes indexing, the on-chain registry, and medal claim status.

ready.title: Ready To Claim
ready.description: These medals have already crossed the threshold. The on-chain claim path is live now.
ready.empty: No medals are ready to claim right now.

progress.title: In Progress
progress.description: The threshold is not full yet, but the system is continuously recording your Frontier trail.
progress.empty: There are no medals in progress. The current set is either already bound or not initialized yet.

bound.title: Already Bound
bound.description: These honors are already bound to the current wallet and no longer exist only as page state.
bound.empty: The current wallet does not have any bound medals yet.

mock.title: Run the demo in three steps
mock.body: All on-chain actions are mocked, but the visual feedback, share cards, and social jumps still behave like the real flow.

one.title: Click Mint Medal
one.detail: Start with Galactic Courier and mint the medal in mock mode.

two.title: Click Share Card
two.detail: Open the share card and flip between X, OG, and Discord formats.

three.title: Click a platform action
three.detail: Jump directly into X, Telegram, or Discord share flows.

hero.title: pilot chronicle
hero.body: This is not just a medal list. It translates Frontier activity into explainable thresholds, evidence, and on-chain claim state.

claimReady.label: Claim Ready
claimReady.ready: Some medals already meet the on-chain claim condition
claimReady.empty: No new medals are ready to claim

indexedPages.label: Indexed Pages
indexedPages.limited: The current scan has reached its page limit
indexedPages.complete: The current scan range has been indexed

lastTrace.label: Last Frontier Trace
lastTrace.detail: The latest Frontier action captured by Chronicle

recommendation.body: Start in Ready To Claim so already-qualified medals do not sit idle.

evidence.title: Show the behavior evidence before the medal state.
evidence.body: Players should first understand which Frontier traces were captured, then decide whether to keep pushing the threshold.

combat.label: Combat Trace
combat.detail: Combat medals only count killmail attackers confirmed by the indexer.

infrastructure.label: Infrastructure Trace
infrastructure.detail: Builder medals pass when the player anchors either 1 network node or 3 storage units.

transit.label: Transit Ledger
transit.detail: Gate jumps are the clearest Frontier signal for logistics and route activity.

detail.bound: This medal is already bound to the current wallet.
detail.mintReady: Threshold complete. Public mint is available now, so this medal can be written directly into the wallet.
detail.claimReady: Threshold complete. The on-chain claim path is live and ready to execute.
detail.verified: The threshold is already satisfied, but the on-chain bind route is not active yet.
detail.tracking: The threshold is not full yet. Keep leaving verifiable Frontier traces.
